clauses.description: The duration for which information must remain confidential.
clauses.standard_guideline: Standard confidentiality duration is 3 to 5 years after termination of the agreement.
risk_triggers: Perpetual confidentiality obligations for standard business information (unless it concerns Trade Secrets).
Obligations shorter than 2 years for sensitive IP or proprietary technology.

clauses.description: Exceptions under which receiving party can disclose confidential information.
clauses.standard_guideline: Standard is to permit disclosure to representatives, affiliates, and advisors who need to know and are bound by confidentiality, or as required by law.
risk_triggers: Lack of standard legal compulsion exception (obligation to comply with subpoenas without liability).
Inability to share with standard legal/financial advisors.

clauses.description: What happens if confidentiality is breached.
clauses.standard_guideline: Injunctions and equitable relief without the necessity of posting a bond.
risk_triggers: Liquidated damages or heavy predefined financial penalties.
Uncapped liability specifically for simple, unintentional breaches (should be standard liability caps or equitable relief).

clauses.description: Which state/country law governs and where disputes are settled.
clauses.standard_guideline: Neutral or mutual jurisdictions like New York, Delaware, or California.
risk_triggers: Unfavorable, remote, or foreign jurisdictions (e.g., specific foreign courts with high cost and risk).
Exclusion of standard waiver of jury trial (sometimes preferred).

clauses.description: Cap on financial liability of either party in case of damage or breach.
clauses.standard_guideline: Liability capped at the total amount paid in the 12 months preceding the claim. Mutual cap is preferred.
risk_triggers: Uncapped liability for standard breaches of agreement.
One-sided cap where only the service provider is capped but customer is uncapped.
Unusually low liability cap (e.g., $100 flat) for critical services.

clauses.description: Duty of one party to compensate the other for losses from third-party claims.
clauses.standard_guideline: Provider should indemnify customer for IP infringement claims. Customer should indemnify provider for customer data infringement or misuse.
risk_triggers: Broad, general indemnifications for negligence without limitation.
No IP infringement indemnity from the SaaS provider.
Obligation to indemnify for indirect or consequential third-party damages.

clauses.description: Commitment regarding service uptime and remedies for downtime.
clauses.standard_guideline: 99.9% uptime commitment with service credits as the sole and exclusive remedy for failure.
risk_triggers: No uptime commitment or SLA whatsoever.
No remedies or credits for significant service outages.
Downtime definition that excludes all maintenance windows without limits.

clauses.description: Right of a party to terminate the contract without cause.
clauses.standard_guideline: Standard is to permit termination for convenience with 30-90 days written notice, or no termination for convenience during the initial term if it is a multi-year commitment.
risk_triggers: One-sided termination for convenience where the provider can terminate at any time, but customer cannot.
Short termination notice periods (e.g., less than 10 days) by provider.

clauses.description: Restriction on employee working for competitors after leaving.
clauses.standard_guideline: Reasonable duration (typically 6-12 months), limited geographic scope, and limited to direct competitors.
risk_triggers: Duration exceeding 12 months.
Nationwide or worldwide geographic scope without clear justification.
Overly broad definitions of competitor that prevent any employment in the industry.

clauses.description: Ownership of creations developed by the employee during employment.
clauses.standard_guideline: Standard IP assignment of work created during employment and relating to the company's business or using company resources.
risk_triggers: Assignment of work created outside working hours, without company resources, and unrelated to company business.
Lack of clean waiver of moral rights.

clauses.description: Compensation paid upon termination without cause.
clauses.standard_guideline: Severance of 1-3 months salary, typically contingent on signing a release of claims.
risk_triggers: No severance provision for termination without cause.
Immediate termination without cause with zero compensation or notice.
